# `sesam doctor` runs the installation health checks and prints a structured
# report + summary. Smoke test only: doctor covers installation, not repository
# integrity (that is `sesam verify`).
#
# doctor exits non-zero here because it expects .gitattributes merge lines that
# init does not write yet (branch-merge support is not implemented), so this
# asserts the report is produced rather than a clean bill of health.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

! exec sesam doctor
stdout '# sesam doctor'
stdout '## System'
stdout '## Git'
stdout '## Summary'
